content:
# Memory Systems in AI Coding Assistants (March 2026)

## Claude Code
- **CLAUDE.md files**: Hierarchical (managed > project > user). `.claude/rules/` for modular rules with `paths:` frontmatter for file-type scoping. Target <200 lines per file.
- **Auto Memory**: Agent-written notes in `~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/`. MEMORY.md entrypoint, first 200 lines loaded at session start.
- **AutoDream** (NEW March 2026): Background sub-agent consolidating memory between sessions (every 24h after 5+ sessions). Converts relative dates, deletes contradicted facts, merges overlapping entries, keeps MEMORY.md under 200 lines.

## Cursor
- No built-in cross-session memory. `.cursor/rules/` only. Had "Memories" feature mid-2025, **removed it late 2025**. Community solutions: Cursor Memory Bank, Recallium, ContextForge (MCP-based).

## Windsurf
- Automatic memory: Cascade AI identifies useful context over ~48 hours. Tracks behavioral patterns, not just files. Limitation: clings to outdated patterns after refactors.

## GitHub Copilot
- **Agentic Memory** (2026): Auto-generated, repository-scoped. 28-day TTL with revalidation reset. Cross-feature sharing (coding agent, code review, CLI). Validates against current codebase before use.
- **Agent Skills** (`.github/skills/`): Procedural knowledge packages with SKILL.md + resources, loaded on demand.

## Open-Source Agents
- **Cline**: Memory Bank methodology (structured MD files). Not built-in — uses custom instructions. MCP variant exists.
- **Aider**: Git-native (every AI change auto-committed). No cross-session memory beyond conventions.
- **Kilo Code**: Had Memory Bank, deprecated in favor of AGENTS.md support.
- **Continue.dev**: Context providers, no native persistent memory. MCP support for external persistence.

## ETH Zurich Study (Feb 2026, arxiv 2602.11988)
- LLM-generated AGENTS.md files **hurt performance by 2-3%** and increased cost 20%+
- Human-written files helped ~4% average improvement
- Recommendation: Limit instructions to non-inferable details only
